acceptance_criteria: A cargo test calling lookup_symbols with name='Foo' against a mock HTTP server returns a typed SymbolLookupResponse with matches populated.
A cargo test calling lookup_symbols with no name/prefix/path raises a compile-time or runtime error before sending an HTTP request (not a backend 400 surprise).
A cargo test calling file_lens with a path containing '/' URL-encodes the path segment correctly (the outgoing request URL contains %2F not bare /).
A cargo test calling symbol_references against a mock returning callers and callees returns SymbolReferencesResponse with both lists populated.

StalenessState struct correctly deserializes both the 'fresh' shape ({ state: 'fresh', fresh: true, indexed_content_hash, indexed_parser_version }) and the 'unindexed' shape ({ state: 'unindexed', fresh: false, detail }).
All public async functions have a one-line doc comment citing the backend route.
nav_receipt_event_id and quiet_background_work_receipt_id are present in every response struct and are preserved (not dropped) through deserialization.
proof_targets: cargo build --package handshake_frontend 2>&1 | tail -5  ->  no errors
cargo test -p handshake_frontend knowledge_code_nav 2>&1  ->  all tests pass
grep -n 'pub mod knowledge_code_nav' src/frontend/backend/mod.rs  ->  line present
grep -n '%2F\|percent_encode\|encode_path' src/frontend/backend/knowledge_code_nav.rs  ->  path encoding present for file_lens route
implementation_notes: The file_lens route path segment requires explicit %2F encoding: the backend decodes it with replace('%2F', '/') so a bare / in the path segment would be mis-parsed by axum's path extractor into multiple segments. Use url::form_urlencoded or manual replace when building the URL.
Symbol entity_id values are opaque strings (format: KE-<uuid>); do not parse them.
The LIST_CAP on the backend is 500; enforce limit <= 500 client-side (clamp, not error) to avoid over-fetching.

Each nav request leaves a KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded EventLedger receipt on the backend (spec 2.3.13.11). The client exposes this receipt ID in the response struct for traceability; consumers should log or store it for audit.
The quiet_background_work_receipt_id comes from the ParallelSwarmStateRecoveryStore; it is present on all six routes. Preserve it even if your immediate consumer does not use it.
Do not fabricate actor_ids or session_ids: call sites must supply them from the current editor session context. Use EditorSessionContext or a similar type passed as a parameter.
The staleness contract: anything not provably fresh (fresh=true) is stale. Never render an unverified symbol as authoritative in the UI.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx - the full React component being ported; shows how symbol_entity_id drives getCodeSymbol, then conditionally loads getCodeFileLens using the staleness.indexed_content_hash and indexed_parser_version fields from the symbol response.
app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.test.tsx - test cases for the symbol panel; provides acceptance-criterion hints for the native widget (to be built in a later MT).

app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts - shows how Monaco integrates lookupCodeSymbols (lookup_symbols route), getCodeFileLens (file_lens route), getCodeSymbolReferences (symbol_references route). Functions configureHandshakeCodeIntelligence and codeSymbolStalenessLabel are reference patterns for the native egui code-lens equivalent.
app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.test.ts - reference tests for staleness label formatting.
app/src/harness/mt249_code_intelligence_harness.tsx - visual harness for the code intelligence integration; reference for what the native pane must display.

risks: file_lens route: bare '/' in path segment mis-parsed by axum into multiple path parts, causing 404. Client MUST percent-encode '/' as %2F when the path contains subdirectories.
lookup_symbols with no name/prefix/path returns backend 400; caller may silently get an error instead of a compilation guard.
StalenessState deserialized to the wrong variant when backend adds new state strings; must not panic on unknown states.
nav_receipt_event_id dropped by a consumer who only reads the payload causes audit trail gap (spec 2.3.13.11 requirement).
Symbol entity_id mis-parsed by client code assuming a specific prefix format causes lookup failures when the format changes.
Over-fetching with limit > 500 causes backend to clamp silently; client may think it got the full result set when it did not.
Stale symbol rendered as authoritative because freshness check is skipped in a fast-path causes user to act on outdated code navigation.
minimum_controls: file_lens path encoding enforced in the client and verified by a cargo test that inspects the outgoing request URL.
LookupRequest type or pre-flight guard ensures at least one of name/prefix/path is non-empty before building the HTTP request.
StalenessState uses #[serde(default)] on optional fields and does not panic on unknown state strings.
nav_receipt_event_id and quiet_background_work_receipt_id preserved in all response structs with #[allow(dead_code)] if not immediately used.
Cargo test validates that StalenessState { state: 'custom_future_state', fresh: false } deserializes without error.
